summary: Crisp soundboard captures a powerhouse first set and an inspired second set with standout Deal, Bird Song and Morning Dew, even though one reviewer noted Jerry’s shaky moments.
review: The recording is a clean SBD that many describe as crispy and very listenable, though not audiophile‑grade. The show delivers a rocking first set highlighted by Deal, Bird Song, Scarlet > Touch and an unforgettable Morning Dew, while the second set shines with Wheel > Other One, Sugar Magnolia and a soaring encore. Most fans love the performance, though one reviewer felt Jerry was barely holding together in the early part of the second set.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews praise both the musical performance and the soundboard quality, with only a single low‑star comment focused on Jerry's playing; overall rating reflects strong show plus good but not perfect recording.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Described as crispy and very listenable; some mention lack of deep low‑end unless system boosts ~125 Hz, and one note it is not A+ but still clear.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set rocks with tight, loose jams; second set is inspired but slightly shortened by heat/humidity, still delivering strong transitions and a powerful encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: One reviewer says he was nearly keeling over in the early second set, while another insists he played fine throughout.
band_member_comments.Phil: His bass is described as trebly but with low end present if the system can boost around 125 Hz.
band_member_comments.Bob: Guitar has a slight out‑of‑tune twang throughout, noted but not detrimental.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum solo leading into Wheel > Other One is praised as totally inspired.
